name: PocketBase
description: Open source backend-as-a-service in a single file with embedded database, auth, and file storage

description.value: Open source backend-as-a-service in a single file with embedded database, auth, and file storage

cms-type.comment: API-first backend with no built-in frontend rendering; primarily a Backend-as-a-Service with CMS-like capabilities

deployment-model.comment: Single executable binary; no official managed hosting

database-support.comment: Embedded SQLite database only; designed for single-server deployments

api-type.comment: REST-ish API with real-time subscriptions via WebSocket

multi-tenancy.comment: Single database per instance; multi-tenancy requires separate instances

static-site-generation.comment: API-only; can be used as a backend for SSG frameworks but doesn't generate static sites itself

content-types.comment: Collections with custom schemas; three types: Base, View, and Auth

custom-fields.comment: 13 field types including text, number, bool, email, URL, select, files, relations, JSON, geopoint

field-types.comment: Editor field for rich text; File field for media; Relation field for references; JSON field for arbitrary data

content-relationships.comment: RelationField supports single and multiple references with back-relation lookups

nested-components.comment: JSON fields can store structured data but no formal nested component/block system

localization.comment: No built-in i18n; must implement manually via custom fields or separate collections

content-validation.comment: Field-type validation, API rules as filters, and custom validation via hooks

content-versioning.comment: No built-in versioning; would need to implement manually

draft-publish.comment: No built-in draft/publish workflow; can implement via custom status field

scheduled-publishing.comment: No built-in scheduled jobs; must use external cron or implement via Go framework

audit-logging.comment: Request logging exists but no content audit trail; can implement via hooks

media-library.comment: File attachments per record; basic management via admin UI

image-transformations.comment: On-demand thumbnail generation with crop modes (WxH, WxHt, WxHb, WxHf)

external-media-storage.comment: S3-compatible storage support (AWS S3, MinIO, Wasabi, DigitalOcean Spaces, etc.)

video-support.comment: File upload supports any file type including video

folder-organization.comment: Files are attached to records; no separate media library with folders

role-based-access.comment: Flexible API rules support role-based patterns via custom fields

granular-permissions.comment: Collection-level CRUD rules (list, view, create, update, delete); no native field-level rules

sso-saml.comment: OAuth2 only (30+ providers); no native SAML support

team-collaboration.comment: No built-in comments, assignments, or notifications

audience-restrictions.comment: API rules can restrict content access based on auth state and custom fields

typescript-sdk.comment: Official JavaScript SDK with TypeScript support

content-type-sdk.comment: Migrations system allows programmatic collection/schema definition

cli-tools.comment: Binary includes serve, migrate, and admin commands

local-development.comment: Single binary runs anywhere; extremely easy local setup

webhooks.comment: Event hooks (Go/JS) can trigger external services; webhook plugin available

plugin-system.comment: Extensible via Go framework or JavaScript hooks (pb_hooks directory)

admin-ui-customization.comment: Admin UI is built-in but not customizable without modifying source

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with examples; comprehensive API reference; active community

frontend-frameworks.comment: No official framework integrations but works with any frontend via REST API; community examples for React, Vue, Svelte, Flutter

search-integration.comment: Basic filtering via API rules; no full-text search integration

response-caching.comment: No built-in API response caching; thumbnail caching exists for images

commercial-support.comment: Community support only; no official commercial support
